name: Drive-Reduction TD Q-Learning (Model-Free)
description: Tabular Q-learning over a joint (grid-position × discretized-hunger) state space where primary reward is defined as the reduction in homeostatic drive. The agent maintains a Q-table indexed by (position, hunger_bin, action) and updates it via temporal-difference learning using homeostatic reward prediction errors (hRPE). Actions are selected via softmax over Q-values. This implements the core Keramati & Gutkin (2014) model-free HRL framework: reward = drive reduction (P1), discounting enforces expeditious correction (P3), and state-dependent reward valuation emerges from the joint state representation (P4). Over time, Q-values converge so the agent anticipates deficits (P5).

variables.name: hunger_level
variables.description: Scalar internal state representing current hunger deficit. 0 = fully sated, higher = hungrier. Increases each step via metabolic drift and decreases when food is consumed.

variables.name: homeostatic_setpoint
variables.description: Ideal internal state the agent seeks to maintain. Fixed at 0 (no deficit).

variables.name: drive
variables.description: Scalar measure of deviation from setpoint: D(h_t) = m * |h_t - h*|^n. Serves as the cost signal; reward is its reduction.

variables.name: homeostatic_reward
variables.description: Primary reward signal: r_t = D(h_t) - D(h_{t+1}). Positive when eating reduces drive, negative when drive increases (e.g., metabolic drift without eating).

variables.name: q_table
variables.description: Action-value function Q(s, h_bin, a). A dictionary mapping (position_tuple, hunger_bin, action_name) -> expected discounted homeostatic reward. Initialized to 0 for all entries.

variables.name: hRPE
variables.description: Homeostatic reward prediction error: delta_t = r_t + gamma * max_a' Q(s', h'_bin, a') - Q(s, h_bin, a). The TD learning signal.

variables.name: external_state
variables.description: Agent's current grid position (x, y), read from perception.

variables.name: hunger_bin
variables.description: Discretized hunger level for tabular indexing. h_bin = int(h_t / h_max * h_bins), clamped to [0, h_bins-1].

variables.name: ate_food_flag
variables.description: Boolean (0 or 1) indicating whether the agent successfully consumed food this step. Read from last_action_result.

parameters.name: drive_exponent
parameters.source: Keramati & Gutkin (2014): quadratic drive penalizes large deviations superlinearly

parameters.name: discount_factor
parameters.source: Keramati & Gutkin (2014): must be < 1 for homeostatic optimality (P3)

parameters.name: metabolic_drift_rate
parameters.source: Per-timestep hunger increase representing metabolic cost

parameters.name: hunger_discretization_bins
parameters.source: Number of bins for tabular Q-learning internal state discretization

rules.description: Drive function — quadratic deviation from setpoint
Internal state dynamics — metabolic drift plus consumption
rules.pseudocode: h_t_next = clip(h_t + lambda_drift - K * ate_t, 0, h_max)

rules.description: Homeostatic reward — drive reduction from state transition
rules.pseudocode:
D_before = m * abs(h_t - h_star) ** n
D_after = m * abs(h_t_next - h_star) ** n
r_t = D_before - D_after

rules.description: Discretize internal state for tabular lookup
Homeostatic reward prediction error (hRPE) — Q-learning variant
rules.pseudocode:
h_bin_next = min(int(h_t_next / h_max * h_bins), h_bins - 1)
max_Q_next = max(Q.get((s_next, h_bin_next, a), 0.0) for a in all_actions)
delta_t = r_t + gamma * max_Q_next - Q.get((s_t, h_bin, a_t), 0.0)
Q[(s_t, h_bin, a_t)] = Q.get((s_t, h_bin, a_t), 0.0) + alpha * delta_t

q_vals = [Q.get((s_t, h_bin, a), 0.0) for a in all_actions]
# Filter: if no food at current position, set q_val for 'eat' to -1e9
if not food_at_position:
    q_vals[eat_index] = -1e9
exp_vals = [exp(beta * q) for q in q_vals]
sum_exp = sum(exp_vals)
probs = [e / sum_exp for e in exp_vals]
a_t = sample_from(all_actions, probs)

decision_logic.description: The agent perceives its grid position and internal hunger state, discretizes hunger into bins, looks up Q-values for all actions in the joint state, applies softmax to select an action (with eating filtered out when no food is present), then after observing the outcome, updates internal state via metabolic drift/consumption, computes homeostatic reward as drive reduction, calculates the hRPE, and updates the Q-table.
pseudocode: food_at_position = any(f['x'] == s_t[0] and f['y'] == s_t[1] for f in food_list)
q_vals = [Q.get((s_t, h_bin, a), 0.0) for a in all_actions]
exp_vals = [exp(beta * (q - max_q)) for q in q_vals]
probs = [e / sum_exp for e in exp_vals]
ate_t = 1 if (action == 'eat' and new_perception.get('last_action_result', {}).get('consumed', False)) else 0
h_t_next = clip(h_t + lambda_drift - K * ate_t, 0, h_max)
# Compute hRPE and update Q (R5, R6)
max_Q_next = max(Q.get((s_next, h_bin_next, a), 0.0) for a in all_actions)
delta_t = r_t + gamma * max_Q_next - Q.get((s_t, h_bin_old, action), 0.0)
Q[(s_t, h_bin_old, action)] = Q.get((s_t, h_bin_old, action), 0.0) + alpha * delta_t

perception_to_variables.food_at_position: any(f['x'] == x and f['y'] == y for f in perception['resources'].get('food', []))

env_mapping.reward_source: Internally computed as drive reduction r_t = D(h_t) - D(h_{t+1}); environment reward from eat action (ConsumeEffect reward=1.0) is NOT used — the agent uses its own homeostatic reward signal.

expected_behaviors.description: Hunger increases monotonically without eating due to metabolic drift
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: run 50 steps with no food in grid → assert h_t increases each step by lambda_drift until h_max

expected_behaviors.description: Eating reduces drive and produces positive homeostatic reward
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: set h_t = 5.0, execute eat at food cell → assert h_t_next < h_t and r_t > 0

expected_behaviors.description: State-dependent reward valuation (alliesthesia): eating near setpoint yields near-zero reward
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: set h_t = 0.1, eat → assert r_t ≈ 0; set h_t = 8.0, eat → assert r_t >> 0

expected_behaviors.description: Q-values converge so agent moves toward food when hungry
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: train 1000 steps → verify agent in high-hunger state selects actions moving toward nearest food more often than away

expected_behaviors.description: Agent does not seek food when sated (low drive → low Q-values for food-seeking)
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: after training, set h_t ≈ 0 → assert agent mostly selects 'stay' or random movement, not directed food-seeking

references: Keramati, M. & Gutkin, B. (2014). Homeostatic reinforcement learning for integrating reward collection and physiological stability. eLife 3:e04811.
Sutton, R.S. & Barto, A.G. (1998). Reinforcement Learning: An Introduction. MIT Press.
Cabanac, M. (1971). Physiological role of pleasure. Science.
